about.quote: From Baoding to Sydney, from code to product, I am finding my own orbit.
about.bio: I’m Gao Xing, MCS candidate at the University of Sydney (2026). My path took me from programming into AI, and from AI into product management — not the most direct route, but every step taught me how technical products go from 0 to 1. I believe great PMs understand both what technology can do and what people actually need.

items.title: Luckin Coffee AI Agent
items.description:
An intelligent operations assistant built with LangChain + DeepSeek, featuring auto-Q&A, user segmentation, and activity recommendations.

The core challenge: private-domain operations suffer from high labor costs and slow response times. By building a RAG knowledge base with an LLM reasoning pipeline, I reduced response time from minutes to real-time while enabling persona-based targeted outreach.

items.title: Crop Classification System
items.description:
A deep learning system using ESA Sentinel-2 imagery and TCN, achieving 0.774 Overall Accuracy across 6 land cover classes.

The core thinking: traditional remote sensing relies on manual labeling and rules with poor generalization. I chose TCN over RNN for parallel computation and longer temporal memory, achieving more stable results with limited labeled data. The project covered data acquisition through evaluation — a complete ML product workflow.

items.title: Personal AI Knowledge Base
items.description:
A knowledge management platform powered by RAG and multi-model collaboration.

The core thinking: information fragmentation is a universal pain point. I designed an “ingest → index → retrieve → generate” pipeline supporting multi-format document ingestion and semantic search, turning knowledge management from folder organization into a conversational agent.
